title: Gate tasks on data readiness, not a timer
description: Trigger downstream work from the actual arrival of upstream data via sensors or task dependencies instead of a fixed schedule offset.
body: Use when a task consumes output that another job or external source produces. Do not start it on a guessed time offset after the upstream is 'usually done' — late upstreams then feed it empty or partial data. Actions: express the dependency explicitly in the DAG, or use a sensor that polls for the data's arrival with a timeout, so the consumer runs only when its input exists. Validation signals include a sensor that blocks until the input lands and a downstream run that never reads partial data. Risk boundary: a timer-gated dependency silently processes incomplete data; if a sensor times out waiting, fail loudly and escalate rather than letting the downstream proceed on missing input.
